mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 9.0 percent signals labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending expectations, generating positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain negative or flat when UNRATE crosses below 9.0, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over 12-month out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter as corporate earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day returns in >60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during high inflation regimes triggers margin compression fears, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >50% of observations when INDPRO rises during CPIAUCSL > 225, or the signal shows no predictive power in high-inflation subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate increase above 0.25 percent signals the end of ultra-accommodative policy, raising discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms sustainable labor market expansion, driving positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a predictive model.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of observations following positive GDPC1 growth, or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production and inflation during post-crisis recovery phases creates policy uncertainty that suppresses equity valuations, yielding negative returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >55% of observations when the divergence occurs, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in the specified regime.
